summary.intent: Fix GitHub issue #655: honor `[skills] enabled = false` config flag at runtime so skills are not injected into LLM prompts when the operator has disabled them.
summary.outcome: PR #663 was created, merged, and all CI checks passed. The fix added a `discover_skills_if_enabled` helper in `crates/chat/src/lib.rs` replacing 4 inline `FsSkillDiscoverer` usages, with two regression tests. A follow-up Greptile comment about reusing an in-scope `config` variable at callsite 2 was also addressed.

repo: CLAUDE.md explicitly forbids `Mutex<()>` as a test serialization primitive; `tokio::sync::Semaphore` with 1 permit is the approved alternative.
CI depends on `local/*` commit statuses published by `./scripts/local-validate.sh`; workflow jobs wait on these statuses and will stay pending/fail if the script hasn't been run for the branch.
Conventional commits (`fix(skills):`) drive automatic changelog generation — no manual docs changes needed for bug fixes that match already-documented behavior.
The worktree path is `/Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/precious-tibia`; the active branch is `precious-tibia`.
moltis-config exposes `set_data_dir` / `clear_data_dir` for test isolation of filesystem-dependent config discovery.

Non-chat callsites (cli, gateway/services, gateway/server, skills/watcher, tools/skill_tools, httpd/server) also call FsSkillDiscoverer unconditionally but were intentionally left out of this PR scope.

code.finding: New `discover_skills_if_enabled` async helper placed after `prompt_build_limits_from_config`; short-circuits to `Vec::new()` when `config.skills.enabled` is false.
Send path callsite — no persona in scope here, uses `moltis_config::discover_and_load()` to get config, matching surrounding patterns.
Model-probe path callsite — `config` was already in scope at ~L4859; Greptile caught the redundant `discover_and_load()` call and it was fixed to reuse the in-scope variable.
load_prompt_persona_for_session path 1 — persona already loaded, uses `persona.config` directly.
load_prompt_persona_for_session path 2 — identical pattern to path 1, uses `persona.config`.

Two regression tests added: `discover_skills_if_enabled_short_circuits_when_disabled` and `discover_skills_if_enabled_runs_discoverer_when_enabled`, serialized via `SKILLS_TEST_DATA_DIR_LOCK: Semaphore` because they mutate the global data_dir override across await points.

workflow: After pushing to a PR branch, `./scripts/local-validate.sh <PR_NUMBER>` must be run manually to publish `local/*` commit statuses before CI workflow jobs will pass.
Greptile performs automated P2-level code review and posts inline comments; threads must be explicitly resolved via GraphQL `resolveReviewThread` after addressing feedback.
Failed CI jobs that were waiting on missing statuses can be re-run with `gh run rerun` once the statuses are published.
The `check-pr` skill was used to fetch and triage PR review comments systematically.

friction: CI jobs silently waited on missing `local/*` commit statuses rather than failing with a clear message, requiring manual investigation of the CI log to discover the root cause.
The `TaskOutput` tool required multiple polling attempts to retrieve the background `local-validate.sh` output.
Clippy flagged `tokio::sync::Mutex` with fully-qualified path as unused-qualifications; required iterative edits to resolve the import conflict.
Rate limit hit mid-session ('You've hit your limit · resets 8pm Europe/Lisbon'), causing an interruption before resolving the Greptile review thread.
open_items: Non-chat callsites that invoke FsSkillDiscoverer unconditionally regardless of `[skills] enabled`: `crates/cli/src/main.rs:495`, `crates/gateway/src/services.rs:236,863`, `crates/gateway/src/server.rs:3887`, `crates/skills/src/watcher.rs:115`, `crates/tools/src/skill_tools.rs:368`, `crates/httpd/src/server.rs:2492` — intentionally deferred to a follow-up issue.
No snapshot/prompt-builder test asserting the `<available_skills>` block is absent from the rendered prompt when skills are disabled — noted as optional during planning but not implemented.
